raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  I was charged $66888.97 by a merchant named XXXX XXXX. I did not receive any service, product, or acknowledgement for this charge. The charge was purportedly for flight tickets, but I booked and paid for the same flight directly with Delta Air Lines immediately after, which has confirmed no record of a third-party booking.

The merchant, XXXX XXXX, is uncontactable (no working phone,  no email, no website, no address) and for intents and purposes is an illegitimate organization and is running a scam(fraud).

I filed a dispute with Citibank. Citibank denied the dispute on the grounds that I originally filed a fraud charge but they responded  that there was "no fraud" because I gave out my card information. Citibank then informed me after several subsequent dispute filings in the next 6 months that since the charge was ruled "not fraud," a billing dispute could not be claimed.

This decision violates the Fair Credit Billing Act (FCBA). The FCBA protects consumers against charges for non-delivered services, which is the nature of this dispute. Citibank is conflating "unauthorized use (fraud)" with "billing error (non-delivery)" to deny my valid claim. I have also filed a police report XXXX XXXX) regarding the suspicious nature of this charge/merchant.

Citibank failed its obligation to conduct a reasonable investigation into a charge from an unverified merchant for a service that was clearly not rendered.

I want the charge of XXXX PLUS INTEREST PAID to be permanently reversed, removed from my account, and the possible negative reporting to all credit bureaus to be immediately ceased.

Attached is the copy of my statement for both the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  charges made on the same date for the same itinerary, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX report, and CITIS RESPONSE.'
